public final TableField<LinkRecord, Long> ID =
    createField(DSL.name("id"), SQLDataType.BIGINT.nullable(false).identity(true), this, "");
Explanation: The table uses a generic 'id' column as primary key while having a unique constraint on 'url' (LINK_URL_KEY), which is a natural key suitable as a primary key since URLs are immutable identifiers for links. Consider using 'url' as the primary key or renaming 'id' to something more descriptive like 'link_id'.

Using selectFrom(CHAT) fetches all columns from the CHAT table, which can retrieve unnecessary data and reduce performance. Consider explicitly selecting only the required columns using select(CHAT.ID, ...).from(CHAT).

Using selectFrom(CHAT_LINK) fetches all columns from the CHAT_LINK table, which may retrieve unnecessary data. Explicitly specify only the required columns using select(CHAT_LINK.LINK_ID, CHAT_LINK.CHAT_ID).from(CHAT_LINK).

Using selectFrom(CHAT_LINK) fetches all columns from the CHAT_LINK table implicitly. Replace with an explicit column selection like select(CHAT_LINK.LINK_ID, CHAT_LINK.CHAT_ID).from(CHAT_LINK) to only retrieve the needed fields.

Using `returningResult()` without specifying columns returns all columns from the LINK table, which fetches unnecessary data. Specify only the required columns explicitly, e.g., `.returningResult(LINK.ID, LINK.URL, LINK.TYPE)`.

Using `selectFrom(LINK)` fetches all columns from the LINK table, which may include unnecessary data. Replace with an explicit column selection like `select(LINK.ID, LINK.URL, ...)` to fetch only the required fields.
Using `select(LINK)` fetches all columns from the LINK table. Specify only the columns you need, e.g., `select(LINK.ID, LINK.URL, LINK.TYPE)` to avoid fetching unnecessary data.
Using `selectFrom(LINK)` fetches all columns from the LINK table. Replace with explicit column selection like `select(LINK.ID, LINK.URL, ...)` to retrieve only the necessary fields.
